name: Netherhampton
coordsSource: web-verified:GENUKI SU108298;Wikipedia/Wikidata grid SU110298 (~150m); listing-centroid n=6 ~1.4km E; 2026-06-04
overview: Netherhampton is a village and civil parish immediately west of Salisbury in Wiltshire, about 2 miles from the city centre, with a population of around 493. Its northern edge borders the water meadows of the River Nadder, and to the south lie Salisbury Racecourse (one of England's oldest, racing since the 16th century), the Salisbury and South Wilts Golf Club and a garden centre. The village was recorded in Domesday and is where the important Salisbury Hoard of Bronze and Iron Age metalwork was excavated in 1993.
character: A historic, rural village right on Salisbury's western edge, combining water-meadow scenery and racecourse/golf amenities with quick access to the city.
amenities: The Victoria and Albert pub (17th-century cottage)
St Catherine's Church (18th-century tower; rebuilt 1876-7 in flint-and-stone chequer)
Salisbury and South Wilts Golf Club

schools.name: Salisbury secondary and grammar schools (Bishop Wordsworth's, South Wilts)

prices.summary: A desirable village on Salisbury's edge; period houses (including the historic Netherhampton House) and family homes, with newer development and a business park in parts. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Golf at Salisbury and South Wilts Golf Club
Water-meadow and River Nadder walks (Netherhampton Circular)
Easy trips into Salisbury and to Wilton House
pros: Rural village feel within 2 miles of Salisbury
Racecourse, golf club and garden centre on the doorstep
Good bus links to the city
cons: No school or shop within the village itself
No railway station; rely on Salisbury
Some modern development and a business park in the parish
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a historic, rural village right beside Salisbury with racecourse and golf amenities close by.
